creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
videos.summary: WorkOS built Studio, an internal workspace where non-technical users can ask business questions in natural language against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion and receive answers or reusable widgets. The LLM generates declarative JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, making subsequent runs deterministic and cost-effective.

videos.title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
videos.summary: Tara Agyemang presents WebMCP, a proposed web standard that enables AI agents to interact with web applications through structured tools instead of screen scraping. The standard offers both declarative HTML attributes for simple forms and imperative JavaScript APIs for complex workflows, with early preview in Chrome 146.

videos.title: How to Keep Shipping When You Walk Away from Your Desk — Zack Proser, WorkOS
videos.summary: Zack Proser presents a four-layer architecture for sustainable AI-assisted development: signal agents monitoring Slack and Linear, verification gates from linting to critic passes, weekly agent analysis of conversation history to surface inefficiencies, and integration of health metrics via Oura ring. The approach treats human attention as the bottleneck and uses voice-first workflows and remote control to enable developers to ship code while away from their desk.

videos.title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel
videos.summary: Small finetuned models with tool discipline and self-correction outperform much larger models on structured query tasks. Training smaller models on tool use patterns with reinforcement learning cost under $500 and achieved better accuracy than Qwen 3 235B on database queries and FinQA reasoning benchmarks.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Cole Medin discusses the US government ordering Anthropic to withdraw Claude Fable 5 from availability due to export-control concerns and a claimed jailbreak vulnerability. The video covers the regulatory action, why the model was pulled overnight, and its performance benchmarks relative to Claude Opus 4.8.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Cole Medin demonstrates Google's open-source Agents CLI, a tool that simplifies building and deploying AI agents to production with minimal effort. The CLI provides agent identity management, sandboxed code execution, and comprehensive audit trails. The tool works with any AI coding assistant and enables rapid development and deployment workflows.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin hosts a live AI Q&A session covering topics like AI coding, agents, Claude Code, and Archon. The session doubles as casual conversation mixed with chess playing. Content is streamed Saturdays and published Wednesday on YouTube.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: The Workflow to Build BEAUTIFUL Frontends
videos.summary: Cole Medin demonstrates a multi-model workflow that routes planning and reasoning tasks to Claude Opus while using Gemini 3.5 Flash for frontend design and UI generation. The approach leverages handoff documents to coordinate between models, treating each phase as a specialized task routed to the model best suited for it.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video explains how to construct proactive agents and self-improving company systems using AI loops. Covers implementation of autonomous feedback mechanisms with application examples in ad optimization and SEO.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses the /goal feature and goal-buddy functionality in what appears to be an agentic coding framework. The video covers setup, prompting practices, and mission exploration for longer horizontal coding tasks, positioning these as steps toward true autonomous coding.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: OpenAI Symphony is a new AI coding paradigm that includes harness setup, automated testing, and workflow management. The video demonstrates how to set up and use Symphony for AI-assisted development.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses autonomous agents and their capabilities, showcasing autoresearch and autoagent functionality. The video covers memory systems in Claude Code, self-evolution mechanisms in Hermes, and skill comparison in OpenClaw.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 in three sizes: Nano, Super, and Ultra. The models combine hybrid Mamba transformer, Mixture of Experts, and MTP architecture for improved performance in language modeling tasks.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax M3 is a new language model that implements MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) instead of full attention. It includes optimizations for KV cache and tiled I/O improvements that reduce inference latency during prefill and decode stages.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code explains Pi, an agent framework that enables unique ways to harness LLMs. The video compares Pi against frontier agents like Claude, Codex, Cursor, and Antigravity, discussing how harness complexity increases as products ship with more integrated features.

videos.title: Huawei makes 1.4nm chip??
videos.summary: Caleb discusses Huawei's announcement of Tau Scaling Law as a competitive response in the hardware and semiconductor race between China and the USA. The analysis emphasizes the roles of EUV and DUV lithography, packaging, and logic folding in advancing chip manufacturing capabilities.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers video discusses strategies for tech job hunting in an AI-driven market, emphasizing networking, personal branding, and open source contributions over mass applications. The content argues that junior developers remain valuable despite AI automation and that job roles evolve rather than disappear entirely.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: Five production deployment patterns for AI agents: streaming tool call events for responsive UI, layered authentication with API Gateway and Cognito, type-safe database access with tenant isolation, guardrails with cost budgets and model routing, and observability via metrics and distributed tracing. These patterns are demonstrated in TypeScript using AWS services and the Strands Agents SDK.

videos.title: Infrastructure as Code for Beginners (AWS CDK)
videos.summary: A YouTube tutorial on Infrastructure as Code using AWS CDK with Python. The video demonstrates building a serverless REST API by first manually configuring DynamoDB, Lambda, and API Gateway through the console, then rebuilding the entire system using CDK with a single deploy command to highlight the benefits of IaC.

videos.title: Stop AI Hallucinations With These 5 Techniques
videos.summary: AWS Developers presents five techniques to reduce AI hallucinations in agent systems: semantic tool selection to filter context, Graph RAG for precise multi-hop reasoning, multi-agent validation to catch silent failures, symbolic guardrails embedded in code, and runtime steering for self-correction. The techniques reduce token waste while improving accuracy and reliability.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech explores whether Google engineers adopt "vibe coding" practices compared to competitors. The video compares code generation vibes at OpenAI and Claude, then examines Google's approach across 2024-2026 timelines and Google Tech Island initiatives.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech examines Malus, a company or tool, and discusses its role in changing open source software dynamics. The video explores tensions between maintainers and commercial actors in the open source ecosystem.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's performance to their own coding ability on a job task. The video documents what worked, what did not, workflow integration, terminal use, and comparative results between AI and human output.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Pigment is an AI performance management platform that integrates finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data for enterprise decision-making. Co-founded by Eléonore Crespo, the company has raised over 400 million dollars and counts Figma, Brex, and Anthropic as customers, growing 2x year over year.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that real product-market fit is marked by customer pull rather than push, and that founders should validate PMF through embedded customer relationships rather than formal interviews. He emphasizes that passionate early adopters can deceive founders into mistaking niche enthusiasm for genuine market fit.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird, founder of Koah Labs, argues that ad-based monetization rather than subscriptions is the future for AI tools and applications. He contends that the primary bottleneck for AI adoption is human trust and adoption timelines, not technological limitations.

videos.title: I Hit $1M ARR in 117 Days. Bootstrapped to $10M. Here's My Playbook | Chatbase, Yasser Elsaid
videos.summary: Yasser Elsaid, founder of Chatbase, shares his playbook for bootstrapping to $10M ARR without external funding. The episode covers reasons to bootstrap, strategies for hitting $1M ARR in 117 days, differences between early and later growth stages, churn reduction, product-led growth, SEO and AEO strategy, outbound tactics, pricing experiments, and decision-making frameworks.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
videos.summary: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

videos.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
